description: Dynamic bridge — 2026-04-30 (Thursday) session | SCORING DAY: P030/P031/P032/P033 | Maharashtra Day May 1 holiday

market_regime.label: GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_EXTREME_CRUDE_ELEVATED_RESISTANCE_ZONE
market_regime.key_risk: Brent at $110-115 — EXTREME_ELEVATED zone continues (Hormuz closure Week 9). UAE exited OPEC effective May 1 — long-term bearish crude signal but Hormuz dominates near-term. Nifty hit 24,334 intraday Apr 29 but closed 24,177 — resistance at 24,200-24,320 (50-DMA) confirmed. BankNifty +0.01% (flat) despite Nifty +0.76% — structural banking weakness persists (RBI ECL framework headwind).
market_regime.dominant_risk: (1) Brent $110-115 — EXTREME_ELEVATED; regime change threshold $95, still $15+ away. (2) Nifty 50-DMA resistance at 24,200-24,320 — faded from 24,334 intraday to 24,177 close. (3) BankNifty structural weakness — ECL framework + HDFC Bank NIM compression + no BankNifty participation in Apr 29 rally. (4) May 1 Maharashtra Day holiday — P031/P032/P033 score on April 30 close.
market_regime.crude_note: Brent closed ~$110.69 (slightly down from $111.26 prev close) but hit $114.60 intraday on UAE OPEC exit news + Hormuz closure. Long-term Brent outlook shifts bearish on UAE supply, but Hormuz closure near-term keeps it elevated.
market_regime.banknifty_note: Essentially flat on Apr 29 (+0.01%). RBI ECL framework structural headwind. BankNifty needs reclaim of 56,000+ for bullish setup.

uae_opec_exit.impact: Long-term bearish crude (UAE spare capacity ~1M bpd+ can be deployed post-Hormuz). Near-term: already priced in on Apr 29. Short-term Brent still driven by Hormuz closure.
uae_opec_exit.india_implication: Positive for India as UAE is key bilateral partner — potential preferential supply terms post-conflict

bajaj_finance_q4.impact: Positive for NBFC sector sentiment. Key catalyst for Apr 29 rally. Provisions did NOT surge as feared.

key_drivers: GIFT Nifty at 24,117 (~-0.25% vs Nifty cash close 24,177) — muted start expected per 5paisa pre-market
Brent rose to $114.60 intraday on April 29 — EXTREME_ELEVATED zone maintained; Hormuz closure Week 9 continues
UAE OPEC exit effective May 1 already priced in on April 29 — no fresh catalyst from same event
April 29 intraday fade from 24,334 to 24,177 (157 pts) — resistance at 24,200-24,320 (50-DMA) confirmed, likely to cap opening bounce
P031/P032/P033 SCORING DAY — May 1 is Maharashtra Day (NSE holiday), so scores effective on April 30 close
Assembly election exit poll results — possible volatility catalyst (state election results)

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent breakthrough to $118.03 (+6% on Apr 29) on Trump blockade extension is REGIME-CHANGING — pierces forecast band ($109-116) and triggers EXTREME_RISK_OFF threshold. Nifty gap-down opening near 23,966 puts P031 (24,050-24,300) trajectory at SERIOUS RISK — Nifty must rally +0.4% from open to enter range. Compounding: US Dow -0.68%, FII Apr 29 net seller -₹2,186 cr (8th+ consecutive session), DII support at risk if crude sustains $118+. Sectoral: OMCs (BPCL, IOC, HPCL, MRPL) face GRM compression at $118 Brent; aviation (INDIGO) jet fuel pressure; defense (ASTRA, MAZDOCK, COCHIN) sustain premium. BankNifty likely flat-down — ECL framework continues structural headwind. Base case: gap-down extends midday, late-session DII bounce to close 23,800-24,000 range.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $115-122 — Brent broke above $116 EXTREME_RISK_OFF threshold to $118.03. Trump explicit blockade rhetoric = sustained premium. Break >$122 = panic regime; break <$112 = relief signal. Base case stays $116-120 today.

scoring_events.P030: ASTRA MICRO ₹1,010-1,060 — SCORE TODAY (close). Apr 29 close ₹1,108.25 — ABOVE RANGE by +4.6%. EXPECTED WRONG. Defense premium reinforced by Brent $118 / escalation.
scoring_events.P031: NIFTY 24,050-24,300 — May 1 holiday → effective score on Apr 30 close. Pre-market 23,966 BELOW RANGE by 84 pts. Needs +0.4% rally from open for CORRECT verdict. PROBABILITY OF CORRECT DOWNGRADED from ~60% to ~25-30%.
scoring_events.P032: BANKNIFTY >56,500 — score on Apr 30 close. At 55,403 needs +2% in one session = highly improbable. EXPECTED WRONG.
scoring_events.P033: COCHINSHIP ₹1,720-1,800 — score on Apr 30 close. Last close ~₹1,695. Defense premium boost from Brent $118 + Trump escalation could lift +1.5-3%. PROBABILITY OF CORRECT UPGRADED from 25% to 35-45%.

scenarios.name: CRUDE_BREAKOUT_EXTENSION (45%)
scenarios.description: Brent stays $116-120. FII selling extends to 8th+ session. Nifty fails to recover gap, drifts to 23,700-23,850. P031 misses (BELOW RANGE). BankNifty breaks 55,000. OMCs sell off -3-5%. Defense (ASTRA, COCHIN, MAZDOCK) +1-3%.

scenarios.name: GAP_DOWN_RECOVERY (30%)
scenarios.description: DII steps in at gap-down lows ~23,950. Bargain hunting in NBFC (Bajaj Finance momentum) + FMCG. Brent stalls at $116-118. Nifty recovers to close 23,950-24,100. P031 marginally CORRECT if close >24,050.

scenarios.name: ESCALATION_SPIRAL (15%)
scenarios.description: Iran retaliates militarily for Trump blockade. Brent spikes >$125. Rupee breaks 95. Defense surges +3-7%, OMCs/aviation -5-8%. Nifty breaks 23,600 to 23,400-23,550 range. India VIX +15%+.

scenarios.name: TRUMP_PIVOT_RELIEF (10%)
scenarios.description: Surprise: Trump signals deal openness OR Iran offers face-saving Hormuz partial reopen. Brent crashes -5% to $112-114. Short-covering rally — Nifty closes 24,100-24,250. P031 CORRECT.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Stalemate probability raised 20→30 after weekend passed without escalation strike AND without any deal restart. Brent cooled to $101.03 despite stalled talks suggests market pricing in narrow stalemate path. Collapse probability nudged 45→40 (fewer fresh negative catalysts overnight); extension 35→30 (talks halted by Trump, no restart in sight).

BAJAJ FINANCE: Post-results consolidation or continuation? Apr 29 was strong (+0.76% Nifty). Watch NBFC sector.
NIFTY 24,050-24,300: P031 SCORE DAY. Must close ABOVE 24,050 for CORRECT. Key support: 24,000 psychological level.
MRPL/OMC: Brent $110-115 sustained — GRM margin squeeze. P035 (₹190-200 by May 25) under pressure. P017 (₹200-210 by May 8) likely WRONG.
SETFGOLD: P034 (₹125-130) — haven bid with Brent elevated. Apr 29 gold -0.81% ($4,554). Watch haven demand vs haven unwind.
INDIGO: Brent $110-115 = ATF pressure. P006 (underperform Nifty 8-15%) direction correct — track cumulative differential.

Compare against actual NSE open vs Nifty prev close 24,177.65. (2) CRITICAL SCORING: P030 ASTRA MICRO — score at Apr 30 close vs target ₹1,010-1,060 (EXPECTED WRONG, stock ~₹1,096+). P031 NIFTY — score at Apr 30 close vs 24,050-24,300 (EXPECTED RIGHT, current 24,177 — IN RANGE; May 1 holiday). P032 BANKNIFTY — score at Apr 30 close vs >56,500 (EXPECTED WRONG, current 55,403). RELIEF_RALLY scenario (15%) materialized via Bajaj Finance Q4 beat + UAE OPEC exit signal. (5) P031 is the key positive watch — Nifty at 24,177 already IN RANGE for 24,050-24,300 target.

scoring_queue.note: Score day TODAY. IRFC at ₹103.36 (Apr 24 data; Apr 28 likely ~₹101-105 given market weakness). Target ₹85-95 ceiling missed by ₹8-19. WRONG outcome breaks 1-RIGHT-in-10-predictions streak (P028 was CORRECT Apr 28).
Apr 28 close ₹1,196.00 vs <₹1,430 target. CORRECT. First RIGHT since P019 (Apr 15). Broke cold streak.
Still ~6% above ceiling. M012 v5 defense floor protecting. Apr 28 market weakness may have compressed price — watch closely.
Nifty closed 23,995.70 on Apr 28 — below range floor (24,050) by 54 pts. Needs +0.23% to enter range. Brent $111 makes this challenging. Two sessions left (Apr 29 + May 1).
BankNifty at 55,400 — lost 56,000 again on Apr 28. Needs +1,100 pts (+2%) in 2 sessions. ECL framework = additional headwind. P038 (fails 57,000) tracking well.
Needs +5-10% in 2 sessions. Brent $111 = defense premium revival scenario possible. Escalation rally path exists if geopolitical escalation triggers defense bid.

opening_bias_actual.gift_nifty_note: GIFT Nifty 23,966 (-0.60% vs own prev close 24,110; -0.87% vs Nifty cash close 24,177.65). Brief generated 08:41 IST pre-market — Nifty open expected near 23,950-24,000.
opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN -0.25% (GIFT 24,117). Actual GIFT Nifty 23,966 = -0.87% gap (211 pts). Direction CORRECT (both gap down). Magnitude 3.5x stronger than forecast — driver: Brent surged to $118.03 (+6%) on April 29 close after Trump confirmed continuing Iran blockade until nuclear deal. Beyond forecast Brent band ($109-116). US Dow -0.68% overnight. UAE OPEC exit + Hormuz Week 9 + Trump escalation = wider gap than EOD wrap modeled.

session_direction_accuracy_today.direction_note: Forecast BEARISH_VOLATILE (morning-brief revision, -0.6%, range 23,900-24,220). Actual: Nifty closed 23,897.95 (-1.14% from 24,173.05). Direction CORRECT (bearish confirmed). Close 23,897.95 is 2 pts BELOW range low (23,900) — essentially AT the forecast range edge. Magnitude ~1.9x stronger than -0.6% base case; actual fell into the ESCALATION_SPIRAL scenario range (23,850-23,950, 15% prob). NOTE: Escalation scenario trigger (Brent >$110) did NOT fire — Brent actually cooled -4.15% intraday to $100.71. Driver was IT sector cascade (INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% follow-through) + weekend risk-off positioning, not geopolitical re-escalation.
session_direction_accuracy_today.range_note: Close 23,897.95 vs forecast range 23,900-24,220. 2.05 pts below range low — ESSENTIALLY AT edge. Counts as marginal range breach (within 0.01%).
session_direction_accuracy_today.magnitude_note: Forecast -0.6% base, scenarios spanned -0.3% to -1.3%. Actual -1.14% falls in escalation-spiral scenario but exceeded base case by ~0.54pts.
session_direction_accuracy_today.brent_note: Forecast Brent $103-108 sustained. Actual $100.71 (-4.15% intraday). Brent forecast WRONG on direction — cooled rather than stayed elevated. Yet Nifty still sold off — key insight: session was dominated by IT sector cascade, not crude macro. FORECAST_PAT007 candidate: idiosyncratic sector drag overrides macro thesis.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scoring_note: Direction CORRECT (bearish). Close marginally below range low (2 pts, 0.01%). Magnitude ~1.9x base case but within scenario envelope. Brent forecast WRONG on direction. Overall PARTIAL_CORRECT — the session-direction call held but macro driver attribution was off.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scenarios_hit: ESCALATION_SPIRAL range (23,850-23,950) — close 23,897.95 sits squarely inside. Scenario was assigned 15% prob; reality triggered via IT cascade rather than Brent spike (different mechanism, same price path).

session_direction.key_assumption: Nifty consolidates after +0.76% rally. 50-DMA resistance capping upside. Brent headwind.

key_drivers: Friday close 23,897.95 (-1.14%) below psychological 24,000 — pressure persists
Weekend BINARY event: Israel strike threats vs Iran + Hormuz shipping disruption still active

Brent closed $100.71 (-4.15% intraday) — cooled but remains in elevated $100-108 zone. Weekend spike to $108+ on any fresh headline = -0.5% to -1.0% gap down; pullback <$95 = flat to mildly positive open
FII selling 4th consecutive day (~₹3,254 cr Apr 24) — structural flow overhang
IT sector: INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% Friday. Monday cash market likely to gap down further in IT unless weekend tech guidance improves
Defense basket firm (COCHINSHIP +4.51%) — expect further geopolitical premium bid if weekend headlines adverse
US Fri session + SGX/Nikkei Monday open will shape overnight sentiment — check GIFT Nifty 06:00 IST Monday
opening_bias_forecast.rationale_note: Confidence 2/5 because weekend binary risk (Israel-Iran escalation) can swing open ±1% in either direction. Base case leans mildly negative on continued FII outflow + below-psychological close. Any ceasefire re-affirmation overnight could flip to gap-up.

opening_bias_actual.gift_nifty_note: GIFT Nifty pre-open Apr 27 ~23,920-23,940 zone (estimated from open). GIFT signaled flat-to-mildly-positive open vs. forecast 23,840 estimate (-0.45% miss to upside).
opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_MILD_GAP_DOWN (-0.25%, GIFT est 23,840). Actual gap +0.199% (open 23945.45 vs prev close 23897.95) — GAP_UP direction OPPOSITE of forecast. Override condition partially triggered: Brent cooled below $100 (WTI above $96 = Brent ~$99-100), enabling oversold bounce + DII activation. The override scenario predicted +0.5 to +1.0% gap up but actual was milder +0.20% — markets cautious due to lingering Israel-Iran weekend tensions (Trump halted talks, Iran FM left Pakistan without meeting US). Graded WRONG — direction opposite, magnitude smaller than override scenario. Missed signal: framework correctly mapped override conditions (Brent cooling) but base case underweighted the oversold-bounce probability after 4-day decline (cumulative -2.78%).

overnight_delta_vs_forecast.ceasefire_status: STALEMATE — Trump halted negotiations; Iran FM left Pakistan without meeting US officials Sunday; no deal, no strike confirmed yet
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.hormuz_status: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed; tensions persist but no fresh strike
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.brent_vs_eod: ~-$1 to -$2 vs Apr 25 settle ($105.33). Brent cooled to ~$99-100 zone overnight despite stalled talks — divergence from war-premium thesis.
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.net_effect: Weekend passed without escalation strike. Brent cooled despite stalled talks (positioning unwind / supply expectations). Combined with 4-day oversold conditions, drove modest gap-up open. FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger) candidate did not fire — talks halted not restarted.

session_direction_forecast.key_assumption: Oversold bounce sustains after 4-day cumulative -2.78% decline. DII support firm at 23,800-23,900 (already tested intraday low 23,936). Brent cooled to $99-100 zone reduces import-cost overhang. IT sector stabilization at lows without fresh negative catalyst (HCLTECH, INFY consolidating). HDFC Bank Q4 already digested (NII +3.2% YoY, NIM 3.38% — modest beat on profit, margin compression a yellow flag for BankNifty). Mid-session short-covering capped at 24,150-24,200 by FII overhang and unresolved Israel-Iran weekend binary still latent. Base case: Nifty closes 24,000-24,150 (+0.4 to +1.0% from open). DII-driven recovery, not structural shift.
session_direction_forecast.brent_forecast: $98-103 — base case Brent holds in this band as Iran talks halted but no fresh strike. Break <$95 sustained 2 sessions = constructive structural shift; break >$105 on fresh headline = afternoon reversal risk.

scenarios.name: CONTINUATION_BOUNCE (50%)
scenarios.description: Oversold bounce sustains. DII buying defends 23,940 morning low. Short-covering builds through morning. IT/Banking stabilize. Brent holds $99-101. Closes 24,000-24,150.

scenarios.name: INTRADAY_FADE (30%)
scenarios.description: Morning bounce fades by afternoon as FII selling resumes (5th day risk). HDFC Bank margin compression (3.38% NIM) weighs on banking. IT no recovery. Closes 23,940-24,030 (mild gain or flat).

scenarios.name: ESCALATION_REVERSAL (15%)
scenarios.description: Fresh Iran-Israel headline drops mid-session. Brent re-spikes >$103. Defense ripper, banking/IT smashed. Nifty reverses to red close 23,800-23,940.

scenarios.name: RIPPING_OVERSOLD_BOUNCE (5%)
scenarios.description: Strong short-covering compounds with DII buying. FII covers part of week net short. Brent stays cool. Nifty rips to 24,200+. P031 (24,050-24,300 by May 1) suddenly back in play.

session_direction_actual.brent_note: Brent cooled $4-5 from Apr 25 settle ($105.33) to $101.03 — held in elevated $100-105 zone, regime-change threshold (<$95 sustained) NOT triggered.
session_direction_actual.session_theme: Oversold bounce day-1 plays out cleanly — DII-driven recovery from 4-day -2.78% decline. BankNifty reclaimed 56,000. Sensex outperformed Nifty (+0.83% vs +0.81%). IT sector stabilization at lows. No fresh Israel-Iran escalation; Brent cooled $4 despite stalled US-Iran talks. Framework v6 forecast vindicated — base-case CONTINUATION_BOUNCE scenario materialized.

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast BULLISH (+0.5% from open). Actual: Nifty closed 24,092.7 (+194.75 / +0.81%), open 23,945.45. Direction CORRECT — index closed firmly higher in line with oversold-bounce thesis.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Forecast range 23,900-24,200. Actual close 24,092.7 sits squarely in upper half of range (mid-band 24,050). CONTINUATION_BOUNCE base case (50% probability, range 24,000-24,150) materialized almost exactly as scripted.
session_direction_accuracy.magnitude_note: Forecast +0.5% from open; actual +0.62% from open (close 24,092.7 vs open 23,945.45). Magnitude error +0.12pp — within 50% tolerance band. From prev close 23,897.95: +0.81% (close-to-close), still within forecast band.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Forecast $98-103. Actual Brent $101.03 — squarely in band. Sub-$103 ceiling held; afternoon-reversal risk did not trigger.
session_direction_accuracy.scenario_match: CONTINUATION_BOUNCE (50% base case) — DII defended morning low 23,940; short-covering built through morning; IT/Banking stabilized; Brent held $100-101.
